item.text: I’m going to answer this by querying the Orbit knowledge graph through `orbit tool run orbit.graph.*` from the repo root, then I’ll extract only crates that directly declare `orbit-knowledge` in their own crate `Cargo.toml` dependency sections.

item.text: I’m now querying config-key symbols directly so we can prove the `orbit-knowledge` entries are in crate-level dependency sections, not just inferred from an edge list.

I’ve validated both candidate crate `Cargo.toml` paths in the graph; next I’m computing the dependency consumer list and count directly from `orbit.graph.deps` output to avoid any manual transcription errors.

item.command: /bin/zsh -lc "orbit tool run orbit.graph.deps --input '{}' | jq -r '.crates | to_entries | map(select(.value | index(\"orbit-knowledge\"))) | .[] | \"\\(.key) crates/\\(.key)/Cargo.toml\" , (\"TOTAL=\" + (length|tostring))'"

/bin/zsh -lc "orbit tool run orbit.graph.deps --input '{}' | jq -r '.crates | to_entries | map(select(.value | index(\"orbit-knowledge\"))) | .[] | \"\\(.key) crates/\\(.key)/Cargo.toml\" , (\"TOTAL=\" + (length|tostring))'"
